title: amaranthus blitum subsp. pseudogracilis
intro: Amaranthus blitum subsp. pseudogracilis, also known as pigweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the amaranth family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been linked to various health benefits. The leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, that may help to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Additionally, Amaranthus blitum subsp. pseudogracilis has been used to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress.
